data.title: Insight signs strategic collaboration agreement with AWS
data.description:
• Insight Enterprises (NASDAQ:NSIT) said on Thursday that it has signed a new strategic collaboration agreement with Amazon Web Services to more effectively help clients manage their cloud roadmap, including data and AI strategy.
  • The SCA strengthens Insight’s role as a provider of cloud, data, artificial intelligence, cybersecurity, and intelligent edge solutions across multiple cloud ecosystems.

data.title: RTX's Raytheon awarded $590M production contract for next generation jammer mid-band
data.description:
• Raytheon, an RTX (NYSE:RTX) business, has been awarded a $590 million follow-on production contract from the U.S. Navy for the Next Generation Jammer Mid-Band system.
  • NGJ-MB is a cooperative development and production program with the Royal Australian Air Force (RAAF). The contract includes delivery of shipsets, support equipment, spares and non-recurring engineering support.
  • Work under this contract will take place in McKinney, Texas; Forest, Mississippi; El Segundo, California; and Fort Wayne, Indiana through 2028.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: J-Long Group Limited announces 1-for-10 reverse stock split to regain Nasdaq compliance
data.description:
• J-Long Group Limited (NASDAQ:JL) announced that it will effect a reverse stock split of its ordinary shares at a ratio of 1-for-10, effective as of 11:59 pm on December 9, 2024, in order to regain compliance with the minimum $1.00 bid price per share requirement of Nasdaq.
  • The company's ordinary shares are expected to begin trading on a reverse stock split adjusted basis on Nasdaq as of the open of trading on December 10, 2024.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Sabine Royalty Trust declares $0.3111 dividend
data.description:
• Sabine Royalty Trust  (NYSE:SBR) declares $0.3111/share monthly dividend.
  • Forward yield 5.73%
  • Payable Dec. 30; for shareholders of record Dec. 16; ex-div Dec. 16.
  • See SBR Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Shift4 cut to Hold at Benchmark over CEO uncertainty
data.description:
Benchmark downgraded Shift4 Payments (NYSE:FOUR) to Hold from Buy after the company's CEO, Jared Isaacman, was nominated to be NASA administrator by President-elect Donald Trump. On Wednesday, BofA also cut its recommendation on the stock to Neutral.

"We view Mr. Isaacman’s pending departure as representing the realization of the key person risk that has been one of FOUR’s primary vulnerabilities since it went public four-and-a-half years ago," analyst Mark Palmer wrote in a note to clients.

Isaacman's acceptance of the nomination gives rise to "significant uncertainty regarding the company’s ability to execute its strategy as effectively as it has up to this point," the analyst added.

Although Isaacman didn't explicitly name Shift4 (NYSE:FOUR) President Taylor Lauber as his successor, he said in a letter to employees that he is confident in Lauber and "the team" to execute the company's growth strategy, Palmer noted.

Benchmark's Hold rating on FOUR aligns with the SA Quant rating of Hold and contrasts with the average Wall Street rating of Strong Buy and average SA Analyst rating of Buy.

Shift4 (FOUR) stock rose 1.2% in Thursday premarket trading after dropping 12% on Wednesday.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Kroger Q3 misses top-line estimates; narrows FY24 outlook
Linkers Industries announces pricing of initial public offering and listing on Nasdaq
data.description:
• Linkers Industries (NASDAQ:LNKS) on Thursday announced the pricing of its initial public offering of 1.9M Class A ordinary shares, at a price of $4.00 per Ordinary Share.
  • The Class A Ordinary Shares are expected to begin trading on the Nasdaq Capital Market on December 5, under the symbol “LNKS”.
  • The offering is expected to close on December 6.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds to acquire companies and/or form joint ventures within the value chain of the wire/cable harnesses industry; to purchase machinery/equipment; to promote marketing and set up global sales offices, particularly in the U.S.; and to fund working capital.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Tullow Oil CEO Dhir to step down; shares plunge in London
data.description:
Tullow Oil (OTCPK:TUWLF) (OTCPK:TUWOY) -9% in London trading Thursday after saying CEO Rahul Dhir will leave the company next year "to pursue other business, academic and family interests."

Dhir, who was appointed CEO in 2020, will stay in his role until a date has been determined to ensure a smooth transition, the company said.

Tullow's (OTCPK:TUWLF) (OTCPK:TUWOY) share price fell to nearly four-year lows last week after the company forecast lower than expected cash flow for the full year.

The company has been struggling with production issues at some of its key operated assets, the Jubilee and TEN fields offshore Ghana.

data.title: Dollar General gives conservative guidance for FY24 on hurricane-related costs
data.description:
Shares of Dollar General (NYSE:DG) are inching higher ahead of Thursday’s open after a 5% improvement in sales in the third quarter eclipsed a miss on profits and a more conservative outlook for the remainder of the year.

The impact from back-to-back hurricanes in October led the company to modify its FY24 guidance to incorporate hurricane-related expenses of $32.7M and a Q4 negative impact of ~$10M. Net sales are now expected to increase by 4.8% to 5.1% versus the previous estimate of 4.7% to 5.3% growth.

Same store sales growth is expected to be between 1.1% to 1.4% from the prior range of up 1.0% to 1.6%, and earnings of $5.50 to $5.90 per share from $5.50 to $6.20, previously. This straddles the consensus estimate of $5.82. Dollar General (DG) also expects to open 730 new stores, remodel 1,620 existing stores, and relocate another 85.

For the current reported quarter, Dollar General (DG) same store sales increased 1.3%, contributing to a 5% increase in revenue to $10.2B, with a 29% decline in earnings per share to $0.89, which missed expectations by 6 cents.

Gross profit as a percentage of net sales was 28.8% in Q3, compared to 29.0% last year. This gross profit rate decrease was primarily attributable to increased markdowns, increased inventory damages and a greater proportion of sales coming from the consumables category; partially offset by higher inventory markups, lower shrink and decreased transportation costs.

Additionally, Dollar General (DG) said it will pay a quarterly dividend of $0.59 per share, payable on January 21 to shareholders of record as of January 7.

Shares are up 1.5% in premarket trading and set to trade in the green for a sixth consecutive day. Dollar Tree (DLTR) is modestly higher in tandem, also supported by a 15% premarket rally in Five Below (FIVE).

data.title: Trust Stamp announces pricing of $3M registered direct offering
data.description:
• Trust Stamp (NASDAQ:IDAI) on Thursday said it has entered into a securities purchase agreement with a single institutional investor to purchase 5.6M shares of common stock in a registered direct offering.
  • In a concurrent private placement, the company also agreed to issue and sell unregistered Series A Warrants to purchase up to an aggregate of 5.6M shares of common stock, and unregistered Series B Warrants to purchase up to an aggregate of 4.2M shares of common stock.
  • The combined effective offering price for each share of common stock and the accompanying Series A Warrants and Series B Warrants is $0.54.
  • Each private placement warrant will be exercisable upon receipt of shareholder approval, will expire five years from the initial exercise date, and will have an exercise price of $0.54 per share.
  • The combined gross proceeds to the company from the registered direct offering and concurrent private placement are estimated to be approximately $3 million.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about December 6.
  • Source: Press Release
